steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to present historical analysis to an audience unfamiliar with the concept. That goal requires a clear definition of historical analysis — what it is and what it does. Examples or comparisons can come later, but a definition must come first for an unfamiliar audience.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "An approach to art criticism, historical analysis considers the historical context in which a work was created.": Defines historical analysis directly and precisely. ✓
  • "*Las Meninas*'s depiction of the artist with King Philip IV symbolizes art's historical ties to power.": Gives an example from a historical analysis without ever defining what historical analysis is. ✗
  • "A stylistic analysis of *Water Lilies* might consider how the painting's loose brushwork represents an impressionist style.": Describes stylistic analysis, not historical analysis. ✗
  • "Stylistic analysis differs from historical analysis in that stylistic analysis examines how an artwork's visual features contribute to its overall style.": Contrasts the two approaches but defines stylistic analysis, not historical analysis. An unfamiliar reader still won't know what historical analysis means. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
